x.name: Aaron Levie
x.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
“If AI can make employees more productive, which is widely accepted as fact, then companies are going to want as many productive units of labor as possible. This is a key reason why I am changing my mind.”

This is why jevons paradox is really important to understand with AI right now. 

And counterintuitively, this trend is going to increase as AI gets better. The better AI gets at performing tasks, the more companies can take on those tasks, which leads to hiring more people to do the surrounding work of those tasks.

Think about the small business that can’t afford to build complex software. When AI is only a little good nothing changes for them. When it’s really good they can finally hire engineers that have the impact of 5-10X, so they can finally invest in engineering. 

The sales team that can automate customer intelligence and outbound demand gen will hire more sales people because they have more leads to go after. The marketing team that can now do higher-end video production than before will hire a video editor. And so on.

This is going to happen in more and more surprisingly ways.

digest:
AI Builders Digest — April 26, 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
X / TWITTER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Sam Altman — OpenAI CEO
GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro are now live in the API. Sam kept it brief: "This was a good week. Proud of the team. Happy building!" — understated for what is a significant model release.
https://x.com/sama/status/2047787124846653895

Sam Altman — OpenAI CEO
GPT-5.5 和 GPT-5.5 Pro 现已正式开放 API 接入。Sam 言简意赅："这是个好周。为团队感到骄傲。继续构建！" —— 对于一次重大模型发布来说，一如既往地低调。
https://x.com/sama/status/2047787124846653895

---

Peter Yang — AI Content Creator & Educator (140K+ readers)
Peter tested GPT-5.5 and Codex by prompting them to build a Star Fox game — done in 15 minutes. More impressively, the AI was play-testing its own game for fun. He has a full video showing what else you can make with GPT-5.5.
https://x.com/petergyang/status/2047882568520102226

Peter Yang — AI 内容创作者与教育者（14万+读者）
Peter 用 GPT-5.5 和 Codex 测试了一个挑战：用 15 分钟构建一个星际火狐游戏。更令人印象深刻的是，AI 居然在自行测试自己构建的游戏。他录制了完整视频，展示 GPT-5.5 还能做什么。
https://x.com/petergyang/status/2047882568520102226

---

Ryo Lu — Designer @Cursor
Ryo has switched fully to GPT-5.5 + Composer 2, calling it "the perfect duo for intelligence, speed, and cost." He also shared a tip: use /multitask to break out of the queue and work on multiple things at once in Cursor.
https://x.com/ryolu_/status/2047879353313431649

Ryo Lu — Cursor 设计师
Ryo 已全面切换到 GPT-5.5 + Composer 2，称其为"智能、速度与成本的完美组合"。他还分享了一个小技巧：在 Cursor 中使用 /multitask 可以跳出队列限制，同时处理多项任务。
https://x.com/ryolu_/status/2047879353313431649

---

Josh Woodward — VP @Google Labs, Gemini, NotebookLM
NotebookLM just got smarter: it can now auto-label and categorize sources. A small quality-of-life update that makes managing large research collections significantly easier.
https://x.com/joshwoodward/status/2047795981534847413

Josh Woodward — Google Labs / Gemini / NotebookLM VP
NotebookLM 新功能上线：现在可以自动标注和分类来源。一项提升使用体验的小更新，让管理大型研究资料库变得更加便捷。
https://x.com/joshwoodward/status/2047795981534847413

---

Amjad Masad — CEO @Replit
Replit now lets you import apps directly from Vercel or Lovable with a few clicks. Breaking down the walls between AI coding platforms.
https://x.com/amasad/status/2047747978690232550

Amjad Masad — Replit CEO
Replit 现已支持从 Vercel 或 Lovable 一键导入应用。AI 编程平台之间的壁垒正在被打破。
https://x.com/amasad/status/2047747978690232550

---

Nan Yu — Head of Product @Linear
Two sharp observations on design and AI: (1) All those "weather apps" and "dashboards" on Behance and Dribbble before AI? They were hallucinations — beautiful outputs with no intent behind them. (2) "The core design is about understanding, not output. A design is an intention. Not an image or a prototype."
https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2047751915271020920

Nan Yu — Linear 产品负责人
两个关于设计与 AI 的犀利洞察：(1) 那些 AI 时代之前出现在 Behance 和 Dribbble 上的"天气应用"和"仪表盘"？本质上都是幻觉——漂亮的输出物背后没有任何真实意图。(2) "设计的核心是理解，而非输出。设计是一种意图，不是一张图或一个原型。"
https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2047751915271020920

---

Dan Shipper — CEO @Every
A crisp paradox worth sitting with: "Any AI knows more than any individual human. But any individual human learns faster than any AI." He also shared an AI-generated audio piece from NotebookLM based on a book he's reading — calling the result astonishing.
https://x.com/danshipper/status/2047797874600161334

Dan Shipper — Every CEO
一个值得细细品味的悖论："任何 AI 都比任何一个人类个体懂得更多。但任何一个人类个体的学习速度都超过任何 AI。" 他还分享了 NotebookLM 基于他正在阅读的一本书生成的音频作品，并赞叹其效果令人震惊。
https://x.com/danshipper/status/2047797874600161334

---

Aaron Levie — CEO @Box
Aaron flagged a counterintuitive argument: if AI increases employee productivity, companies want *more* employees, not fewer — because marginal output per person goes up. The debate around this is heating up.
https://x.com/levie/status/2047747142308274645

Aaron Levie — Box CEO
Aaron 提到了一个正在流传的反直觉论点：如果 AI 提升了员工生产力，公司反而会想要*更多*员工，而非更少——因为每个人的边际产出在上升。围绕这一议题的讨论正在升温。
https://x.com/levie/status/2047747142308274645

---

Swyx — AI Engineer & Writer
Two things: (1) Vercel's AI engineering work "comes direct from the C-suite" — a signal that AI infra is now a boardroom priority. (2) "The hot skills of today become the posttrain targets of tomorrow" — whatever you learn now may get automated into models soon.
https://x.com/swyx/status/2047715417867907539

Swyx — AI 工程师与作家
两点值得关注：(1) Vercel 的 AI 工程工作"直接来自最高管理层"——这是一个信号：AI 基础设施已成为董事会级别的优先事项。(2) "今天的热门技能，将成为明天的后训练目标"——意味着你现在学的东西，很快可能会被直接内化进模型里。
https://x.com/swyx/status/2047715417867907539

---

Aditya Agarwal — GP @South Park Commons
One sentence that captures the current moment: "We're approaching a post-prompting world." As models improve, the skill of writing prompts itself may become obsolete.
https://x.com/adityaag/status/2047669585667666321

Aditya Agarwal — South Park Commons 合伙人
一句话概括当下时刻："我们正在走向一个后提示词时代。" 随着模型不断进化，"写好提示词"这项技能本身可能即将过时。
https://x.com/adityaag/status/2047669585667666321

---

Matt Turck — VC @FirstMarkCap
Cohere and Aleph Alpha combining is "such an interesting moment" — Matt notes it's hard to overstate how much AI activity is happening outside the US right now.
https://x.com/mattturck/status/2047721904438493482

Matt Turck — FirstMarkCap 风险投资人
Cohere 与 Aleph Alpha 的合并是"一个非常有趣的时刻"—— Matt 指出，目前 AI 领域的大量活动正在美国以外地区涌现，这一点很难被高估。
https://x.com/mattturck/status/2047721904438493482

---

Peter Steinberger — Builder & AI Tinkerer
Two updates: (1) GPT-5.5 is "definitely a step up in the character game." (2) discrawl 0.6.0 is out — now able to read Discord DMs without custom login tricks, opening up new possibilities for Discord data access.
https://x.com/steipete/status/2047797210427859450

Peter Steinberger — 构建者 & AI 探索者
两则更新：(1) GPT-5.5 在对话体验上"明显更进一步"。(2) discrawl 0.6.0 发布——现在无需自定义登录技巧即可读取 Discord 私信，为 Discord 数据访问打开了新的可能性。
https://x.com/steipete/status/2047797210427859450

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
OFFICIAL BLOGS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Anthropic Engineering: Scaling Managed Agents — Decoupling the Brain from the Hands
Anthropic's engineering team shares a key architectural insight: separate the "brain" (the LLM doing reasoning) from the "hands" (tools and execution). Assumptions baked into agent harnesses go stale as models improve — here's how to build systems that stay durable as capabilities evolve.
https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/managed-agents

Anthropic Engineering：扩展托管 Agent——将大脑与双手解耦
Anthropic 工程团队分享核心架构洞见：将"大脑"（负责推理的 LLM）与"双手"（工具和执行层）分离。随着模型能力不断提升，内嵌于 Agent 框架中的假设会逐渐过时——这篇文章探讨如何构建能随模型演进而保持持久性的系统。
https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/managed-agents

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PODCAST
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

No Priors — SAP CTO Philipp Herzig: AI is a Business Model Transition, Not Just a Technology Transition
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5u7AjPardvo

The Takeaway: Enterprise AI adoption isn't primarily a technical challenge — it's a fundamental business model rethink. SAP's CTO argues that companies treating AI as just another technology upgrade are missing the real disruption.

Philipp Herzig, CTO of SAP (400,000 enterprise customers), explains why SAP has survived every major tech transition — mainframe to cloud to AI — and what's different this time. His core argument: SAP endures because it solves the "standard software" problem that never goes away. Every company still needs finance, HR, supply chain, and procurement managed end-to-end. The insight of standard software — write once, deploy everywhere — has compounded for 50 years.

On AI: "AI is a business model transition, not just a technology transition." The cost of building is now so low that competitive moats shift to data, process, and ecosystem. SAP's advantage is sitting at the center of real business data — the kind that actually runs companies.

He's skeptical of pure LLMs for predictive analytics: language models are great at reasoning, but enterprise forecasting (supply chain optimization, real-world-scale traveling salesman problems) still requires specialized approaches. And entrepreneurs consistently underestimate the challenges of scale — every AI startup solving a vertical problem will eventually face: "Do you rebuild for every customer, or do you build standard software?"

---

No Priors — SAP CTO Philipp Herzig：AI 是商业模式的转型，而非仅仅是技术转型
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5u7AjPardvo

要点：企业级 AI 应用的核心挑战不是技术问题，而是商业模式的根本性重构。SAP CTO 认为，把 AI 当作普通技术升级的企业，正在错过真正的颠覆机会。

SAP CTO Philipp Herzig（SAP 服务 40 万企业客户）讲述 SAP 为何能穿越从大型机到云计算再到 AI 的每一次技术变革，以及这一次有何不同。核心论点：SAP 之所以长青，是因为它解决了一个永远不会消失的问题——"标准软件"问题。每家企业始终需要端到端地管理财务、HR、供应链和采购。"一次开发，处处部署"的洞见已经复利积累了 50 年。

关于 AI："AI 是商业模式的转型，而不仅仅是技术转型。"如今构建的成本已极低，竞争护城河已转移到数据、流程和生态系统上。SAP 的优势在于掌握真实业务数据的核心——那些真正驱动企业运转的数据。

他对纯 LLM 用于预测性分析持怀疑态度：语言模型擅长推理，但企业级预测（供应链优化、现实世界规模的旅行商问题）仍需专门的方法。创业者始终低估规模化的挑战——每一个解决垂直领域问题的 AI 创业公司，最终都会面对同一个问题："你是为每个客户重复构建，还是走标准软件的路？"

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
